back_translation: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
"Make for yourself two trumpets of silver; of hammered work you shall make them; and they shall be for you for calling the congregation and for setting the camps in motion.
And they shall blow with them, and all the congregation shall assemble to you at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.
And if with one they blow, then the leaders, the heads of the thousands of Israel, shall assemble to you.
And you shall blow short blasts, and the camps that camp on the east shall set out.
And you shall blow short blasts a second time, and the camps that camp on the south shall set out; short blasts they shall blow for their setting out.
And to assemble the assembly you shall blow long, and you shall not blow short blasts.
And the sons of Aaron, the priests, shall blow the trumpets; and they shall be for you a permanent statute for your generations.

And when you enter into battle in your land against an adversary who oppresses you, you shall blow short blasts on the trumpets; and you shall be remembered before the LORD your God, and you shall be saved from your enemies.

And on the day of your joy and on your appointed feasts and on the beginnings of your months, you shall blow on the trumpets over your burnt offerings and over your peace offerings; and they shall be for you a memorial before your God; I am the LORD your God."
And it happened in the second year, in the second month, on the twentieth of the month, the cloud was lifted from above the tabernacle of the Testimony.
And the sons of Israel set out according to their settings out from the wilderness of Sinai, and the cloud settled in the wilderness of Paran.
And they set out for the first time, according to the word of the LORD through Moses.
And the standard of the camp of the sons of Judah set out first according to their armies; and over his army was Nahshon son of Amminadab.
And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Issachar was Nethanel son of Zuar.
And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Zebulun was Eliab son of Helon.
And the tabernacle was taken down, and the sons of Gershon and the sons of Merari set out, the bearers of the tabernacle.
And the standard of the camp of Reuben set out according to their armies; and over his army was Elizur son of Shedeur.
And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Simeon was Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.
And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Gad was Eliasaph son of Deuel.
And the Kohathites set out, the bearers of the sanctuary; and they (the Gershonites/Merarites) set up the tabernacle before their arrival.
And the standard of the camp of the sons of Ephraim set out according to their armies; and over his army was Elishama son of Ammihud.
And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Manasseh was Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.
And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin was Abidan son of Gideoni.
And the standard of the camp of the sons of Dan set out, the rear guard of all the camps according to their armies; and over his army was Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.
And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Asher was Pagiel son of Ocran.
And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Naphtali was Ahira son of Enan.
These were the settings out of the sons of Israel according to their armies; and they set out.

And Moses said to Hobab son of Reuel the Midianite, the father-in-law of Moses: 'We are setting out for the place of which the LORD said, "I will give it to you." Come with us, and we will do good to you, for the LORD has spoken good concerning Israel.'
And he said to him: 'I will not go, but to my own land and to my birthplace I will go.'
And he said: 'Please do not leave us, because you know our camping in the wilderness, and you shall be to us as eyes.'
And it shall be, when you go with us, and it shall be — that good which the LORD will do good with us, we will do good to you.'
And they set out from the mountain of the LORD, a journey of three days; and the ark of the covenant of the LORD set out before them, a journey of three days, to seek out for them a resting place.
And the cloud of the LORD was over them by day when they set out from the camp.
And it happened, when the ark set out, that Moses said: 'Arise, O LORD, and let Your enemies be scattered, and let those who hate You flee from before You.'
And when it rested, he would say: 'Return, O LORD, to the ten thousand thousands of Israel.'
